Still, these are minimal concerns.\n\n---\n\n### 4. **Justification for risk scores > 0.5**\n\n- None of the reports assign a risk higher than 0.1, which is appropriate.\n- The minimal code snippet poses virtually no security risk, so higher scores are unwarranted.\n\n---\n\n### 5. **Malware score considerations**\n\n- All reports correctly assign a malware score of 0, consistent with the code.\n- No signs of malicious behavior are present.\n\n---\n\n### 6. **Claims based on assumptions**\n\n- The reports correctly avoid assumptions; they base their conclusions on the code's content.\n- They recognize that the source map comment is standard and not suspicious.\n\n---\n\n### 7. **Consistency of scores with the report**\n\n- The scores are consistent with the minimal, benign nature of the code.\n- The confidence levels are high (0.8–0.9), appropriate for such a straightforward snippet.\n\n---\n\n### **Overall assessment:**\n\n- The code is benign, and the reports' evaluations are accurate.\n- The only minor point is the security risk score of 0.1 in some reports, which could be lowered to 0, but this is a minor issue.\n\n---\n\n### **My own estimates:**\n\n- **Malware score:** 0 (no malicious activity)\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 (clear code)\n- **Risk score:** 0 (no security risk)\n\n---\n\n### **Final notes:**\n\nThis code snippet is a standard build artifact with no security concerns.
